page.content: In the study by Carrard et al., 2011 [49], the sample sizes were 40 for SZ, 58 for BD, and 67 for HC, with mean ages of 32 (8) for SZ, 42 (10) for BD, and 42 (12) for HC, revealing 40% female for SZ, 57% for BD, and 27% for HC. The method utilized was an HRM assay b, c, e, analyzing covariates such as age j, sex j, and symptoms j, concluding that 5-HTR1A g showed differential methylation for SZ/BD compared to HC and for SZ compared to BD. Gradirivasf et al., 2011 [50] examined 24 subjects each for SZ, BD, and HC, with no mean age reported, and employed bisulfite sequencing and qMSP b, d, e, considering covariates including age h, sex j, genotype h, medication (antipsychotics) h, marital status j, smoking j, alcohol abuse j, and education j, finding no differential methylation of HTR2A (promoter) j for most CpG sites except for 1438A/G, 1420, and 1223 polymorphic sites, with hypomethylation of T120C in SZ/BD h and lower methylation in SZ CC allele carriers with increasing age. Li et al., 2014 [51] included 6 for SZ, 3 for BD, and 1 for HC, with mean ages of 24 (7) for SZ and 47 (11) for BD, and 67% female for SZ and 33% for BD, using methylated DNA immunoprecipitation a, c, f, considering age i and sex i, reporting hypermethylation of ADRB1 g, HTR1A g, NPAS1 g, and hypomethylation of COMT h in SZ, with HNRNPA1 and 56 other genes differentially methylated in both SZ & BD i, and 11 genes differentially methylated among SZ & BD i.

for SZ, 42 (10) for BD, and 42 (12) for HC, revealing 40% female for SZ, 57% for BD, and 27% for HC. The method utilized
was an HRM assay b, c, e, analyzing covariates such as age j, sex j, and symptoms j, concluding that 5-HTR1A g showed
differential methylation for SZ/BD compared to HC and for SZ compared to BD. Gradirivasf et al., 2011 [50] examined 24
subjects each for SZ, BD, and HC, with no mean age reported, and employed bisulfite sequencing and qMSP b, d, e,
considering covariates including age h, sex j, genotype h, medication (antipsychotics) h, marital status j, smoking j, alcohol
abuse j, and education j, finding no differential methylation of HTR2A (promoter) j for most CpG sites except for 1438A/G,
1420, and 1223 polymorphic sites, with hypomethylation of T120C in SZ/BD h and lower methylation in SZ CC allele carriers
with increasing age. Li et al., 2014 [51] included 6 for SZ, 3 for BD, and 1 for HC, with mean ages of 24 (7) for SZ and 47 (11)
for BD, and 67% female for SZ and 33% for BD, using methylated DNA immunoprecipitation a, c, f, considering age i and sex
i, reporting hypermethylation of ADRB1 g, HTR1A g, NPAS1 g, and hypomethylation of COMT h in SZ, with HNRNPA1 and
56 other genes differentially methylated in both SZ & BD i, and 11 genes differentially methylated among SZ & BD i.

page.content: Nohesara et al., 2011 [52] studied 20 for SZ, 20 for BD, and 25 for HC, employing bisulfite sequencing and qMSP b, d, e, analyzing covariates including age g, sex, marital status j, and genotype j, finding hypomethylation of MB-COMT (promoter) h and higher methylation in SZ with increasing age. In Table 2, the WHO garnered 82.3% trust a lot, 11.6% trust moderately, 1.8% do not trust, 1.2% not sure, and 3.0% no response, while universities/research institutes received 56.1% trust a lot, 37.8% trust moderately, 1.8% do not trust, 1.2% not sure, and 3.0% no response. International media had 30.5% trust a lot, 37.2% trust moderately, 18.3% do not trust, 3.7% not sure, and 10.4% no response, foreign biotechnology companies had 35.4% trust a lot, 33.5% trust moderately, 20.1% do not trust, 3.7% not sure, and 7.3% no response, religious bodies had 27.4% trust a lot, 30.5% trust moderately, 16.5% do not trust, 12.2% not sure, and 13.4% no response, Nigerian media had 16.5% trust a lot, 36% trust moderately, 29.9% do not trust, 8.5% not sure, and 9.1% no response, and the Nigerian government had 11% trust a lot, 28% trust moderately, 49.4% do not trust, 3.7% not sure, and 9.1% no response.

lines.text: Nohesara et al., 2011 [52] studied 20 for SZ, 20 for BD, and 25 for HC, employing bisulfite sequencing and qMSP b, d, e,
analyzing covariates including age g, sex, marital status j, and genotype j, finding hypomethylation of MB-COMT (promoter) h
and higher methylation in SZ with increasing age. In Table 2, the WHO garnered 82.3% trust a lot, 11.6% trust moderately,
1.8% do not trust, 1.2% not sure, and 3.0% no response, while universities/research institutes received 56.1% trust a lot, 37.8%
trust moderately, 1.8% do not trust, 1.2% not sure, and 3.0% no response. International media had 30.5% trust a lot, 37.2% trust
moderately, 18.3% do not trust, 3.7% not sure, and 10.4% no response, foreign biotechnology companies had 35.4% trust a lot,
33.5% trust moderately, 20.1% do not trust, 3.7% not sure, and 7.3% no response, religious bodies had 27.4% trust a lot, 30.5%
trust moderately, 16.5% do not trust, 12.2% not sure, and 13.4% no response, Nigerian media had 16.5% trust a lot, 36% trust
moderately, 29.9% do not trust, 8.5% not sure, and 9.1% no response, and the Nigerian government had 11% trust a lot, 28%
trust moderately, 49.4% do not trust, 3.7% not sure, and 9.1% no response.
